headline: Penang tunnel project: RM6.3 billion committed, 0% tunnel built
context: The Penang undersea tunnel project has committed RM6.3 billion in public funds since 2013 but zero metres of the actual tunnel have been constructed after 13 years.

cards.big: RM6.3 billion committed to Penang tunnel — 13 years later, 0% tunnel exists
cards.sub: Land reclamation proceeded but the actual tunnel hasn't started construction

cards.big: Contract structured as land-swap deal — developer gets island land in exchange for tunnel
cards.sub: Complex public-private structure obscures accountability for non-delivery

cards.big: Land reclamation generated RM4.8 billion in developer revenue before tunnel construction began
cards.sub: Developer profited from land while the public infrastructure obligation remains unfulfilled

cards.big: MACC investigated in 2019 but case was closed without prosecution in 2024
cards.sub: Investigation produced no accountability despite billions in public commitment

cards.big: A project that delivers profit to developers but no tunnel to citizens isn't a partnership — it's a transfer
Should land-swap infrastructure deals be banned in favour of transparent procurement?
